_meta.source: DAA Ground Operations — Roster Optimisation Constraints
_meta.description: Constraint configuration for Flask-based roster optimisation engine. All time values in minutes unless stated. All flags are boolean.

shifts.description: Two fixed shifts only. No split shifts or custom windows permitted.

working_hours.description: Hard upper limits on working time. Optimiser must not generate rosters that breach these.
working_hours.note: max_shift_hours is already fixed by the two-shift structure above. Weekly cap applies across Mon–Sun.

rest_rules.description: Minimum rest periods between consecutive shift assignments.
rest_rules.note: A staff member finishing the 12:00–00:00 night shift cannot start the 00:00–12:00 day shift until the minimum 11-hour rest is satisfied. Flag a breach if any assignment window violates this.

overtime.description: Maximum permitted overtime beyond contracted hours.
overtime.note: Overtime is defined as any rostered time beyond the contracted 12-hour shift. Breach triggers a warning flag in the optimiser output before hard block is applied at the cap.

breaks.description: Two mandatory breaks per shift. Minimum active working time required before each break can be taken.

first_half_break.label: First Half Break
first_half_break.window: Must fall within first half of shift (first 6 hours). Minimum 2 hours worked before break starts.

second_half_break.label: Second Half Break
second_half_break.window: Must fall within second half of shift (hours 6–12). Minimum 2 hours worked after first break ends before second break starts.

breaks.note: Gross shift = 720 mins. Less 30 min first break and 60 min second break = 630 mins net working time available for task assignment.

skill_assignment.description: Rules governing which skill is used when assigning staff to tasks.
secondary_skill_conditions: No available primary skill holder exists for the task window
All primary skill holders are already assigned or on break
A critical gap exists and no primary holder is reachable within time
skill_assignment.note: Optimiser should maximise primary skill usage. Secondary skill deployment should be logged as a reallocation event in the output for supervisor visibility.

travel_time.description: Additional buffer time added to task windows when a staff member must physically relocate before starting a task.

rules.trigger: Staff member switching from primary skill task to secondary skill task at a different location
rules.note: Add 10 minutes to the end of the current task window before the next task can start. Prevents double-booking across the travel gap.

rules.trigger: Staff member moving from any T1 post to any T2 post
rules.note: Inter-terminal transit. No task assignment should begin at the destination terminal until travel time has elapsed.

rules.trigger: Staff member moving from any T2 post to any T1 post
rules.note: Symmetric with T1_TO_T2. Same buffer applies in both directions.

rules.trigger: Staff member moving between tasks within the same terminal on the same skill
rules.note: No mandatory buffer but optimiser should check pier distance if pier data is available.

double_booking.description: A single staff member cannot be assigned to two overlapping task windows, accounting for travel time.
double_booking.note: Task window overlap check must include travel_time buffer from the travel_time rules above. Two tasks are considered overlapping if task_2.start < task_1.end + travel_time.

absence.description: Staff on any recorded leave type must be excluded from all task assignment on affected dates.
absence.note: Absence data is sourced from the leave register. Absent staff must not appear in any optimised roster output for their leave period.

optimiser_flags.description: Output flags the optimiser should attach to generated rosters for supervisor review.

flags.condition: A CRITICAL priority task has 0 staff assigned
A HIGH priority task is understaffed by 1 or more
flags.action: Warn supervisor. Suggest reallocation from secondary skill holders.

flags.condition: A task has been filled using a secondary skill holder instead of primary
Staff member approaches 80% of any overtime cap
flags.action: Warn. Do not assign further overtime without supervisor approval.

flags.condition: Assignment would leave less than 11 hours rest before next shift
Travel time between two consecutive assignments is insufficient
flags.action: Reject second assignment. Propose next available slot.

flags.condition: Break has not been scheduled within the required window
flags.action: Insert break before next task assignment is made.
